column1: # Alpha-globin deletion/triplication alleles — copy-number signatures
# GENERATED by bin/make_hba_deletion_alleles.py. Do not hand-edit.
# THESE ARE NOT BREAKPOINTS. Alpha-cluster NAHR breakpoints sit inside
# near-identical homology boxes and are published against varying builds;
# hand-typing GRCh38 coordinates for them would be guesswork. Alleles are
# therefore defined by WHICH SEGMENTS THEY REMOVE (see hba_segments.bed),
# which is what a depth caller measures anyway.
# approx_size is DOCUMENTARY ONLY. Never use it as a coordinate.
# basis:  observed   = measured in this project's data, cited in note
#         literature = allele composition is textbook; SIZE is approximate
# d_* columns are the copy change on the AFFECTED chromosome, in
# GENOMIC order, and name the segments in hba_segments.bed:
#   0 intact · -1 lost · + gained
#   h  disrupted/hybrid: rearranged but NOT cleanly lost; still reads as
#      ~2 copies. This is an EXPECTATION to match against, not a wildcard.
#   ?  unconstrained: the expected depth straddles a decision boundary, so
#      this segment MUST NOT be compared for this allele.
# h and ? are NOT interchangeable. Treating -a4.2's INTER_A2_A1 as 'h'
# (= expect 2 copies) misnames a silent 3-gene -a4.2 carrier as a 2-gene
# alpha-thal trait whenever its 5' breakpoint takes enough of the segment.
# INTER_Z_A deliberately has NO column: it is flagged do_not_average in
# hba_segments.bed, so no allele may be defined against it.
# depth_distinguishable=no:A|B means depth ALONE cannot separate those
# alleles. The caller MUST report the group, not arbitrarily pick one.
# Resolving within a group needs the deletion extent or a junction read.
allele
--SEA
column12: OBSERVED in THAL1: normalized depth 0.45-0.60 across chr16:164000-186000, HBZ 1.00, flanks 1.05. Spares HBZ. Commonest 2-gene allele in SE Asia.

column1: --MED
column12: Removes both alpha genes, spares HBZ. Depth-degenerate with --SEA; separating them needs the extent or a junction read.

column1: --FIL
column12: Larger; extends into HBZ. Loss of HBZ signal is the discriminator vs --SEA/--MED.

column1: --THAI
column12: As --FIL, also removes HBZ. Depth-degenerate with --FIL.

Rightward NAHR between Z boxes; fuses HBA2 5' to HBA1 3' into ONE hybrid gene. Neither gene body vanishes cleanly - the deleted 3.7 kb lies between them, so INTER_A2_A1 is the diagnostic segment, not HBA1/HBA2. Most common alpha-thal deletion worldwide.

Leftward NAHR between X boxes; removes HBA2, leaves HBA1 intact. INTER_A2_A1 is '?' (UNCONSTRAINED), not 'h' and not 0: the deletion is ~4.2 kb but HBA2's gene body is only 835 bp, so the X2 crossover lies INSIDE the 2969 bp INTER_A2_A1 and 2-3.4 kb of it goes too. Depending on where the 5' breakpoint falls that segment scores anywhere from ~0.43 to ~0.77 - i.e. it straddles the het-loss cutoff. Constraining it either way would misname a real carrier, so it is not compared. HBA2 lost with HBA1 intact is the call.

column1: anti-3.7
column12: Reciprocal product of the -a3.7 NAHR: alpha-gene TRIPLICATION (aaa/). Gains a gene rather than losing one. Benign alone, but modifies beta-thal severity, and a caller that only looks for losses will silently miss it.
